questionText: Which of the following is the best interpretation of the y-intercept of the line of best fit in the figure? The line crosses the y-axis at a specific point, as shown.
explanation: To find the y-intercept, look for where the line crosses the y-axis. In this figure, the line crosses the y-axis at y = 10. Therefore, the y-intercept is 10.

distractorExplanations.B: This option is incorrect due to an off-by-one-category mistake. The student may have miscounted the units on the y-axis or added an extra unit to the correct y-intercept value. To find the correct y-intercept, simply look for where the line crosses the y-axis and read the value from the y-axis.
distractorExplanations.C: This option is incorrect due to a label-misread mistake. The student may have misread the label on the y-axis or confused it with another point on the line. Make sure to carefully read the labels on the axes and identify the correct point where the line crosses the y-axis.
distractorExplanations.D: This option is incorrect due to a range-as-mode mistake. The student may have looked at the range of y-values covered by the line and mistakenly chosen a value within that range as the y-intercept. Instead, focus on where the line actually crosses the y-axis to find the correct y-intercept.
